html:
[1476]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 弥子瑕 - A proper name, the subject of the first story.
  • 卫之嬖大夫 - Refers to a favored official of the state of Wei.
  • 窃驾君车罪刖 - A legal penalty in the state of Wei for stealing the king's carriage (刖: amputation).
  • 矫驾君车以出 - Refers to the act of driving the king's carriage without permission.
  • 孝哉 - An expression of admiration for the subject's filial piety.
  • 为母之故，犯刖罪 - Indicates that the subject committed a crime out of filial piety.
  • 爱我忘其口，啖寡人 - A metaphorical expression suggesting the subject's love for the king was so great that he even forgot his own mouth.
  • 色衰而爱驰 - Refers to the decline of the subject's favor due to aging and loss of beauty.
  • 得罪于君 - Indicates that the subject incurred the king's displeasure.
  • 尝矫驾吾车，又尝食我以余桃者 - Refers to past actions that the subject had taken against the king's rules.
  • 王韶 - A proper name, the subject of the second story.
  • 王韶字德茂 - Indicates the subject's courtesy name.
  • 少美丽，善姿首 - Describes the subject's physical attractiveness.
  • 袭父封都乡侯 - Refers to inheriting a title from one's father.
  • 为太子舍人 - Indicates the subject's position as a retainer to the crown prince.
  • 累迁郢州刺史 - Refers to the subject's promotion to the position of governor of Jingzhou.
  • 庾开府 - A proper name, likely referring to a high-ranking official or nobleman.
  • 断袖之欢 - Refers to a homosexual relationship.
  • 衣食所资，皆信所给 - Indicates that the subject's material needs were provided by the other party.
  • 接信甚薄 - Refers to the subject's cold reception of the other party.
  • 坐青油幕下 - Indicates the subject's position in a tent.
  • 践踏肴馔 - Refers to the act of trampling on food and drink.
  • 有自矜色 - Refers to the subject's arrogant demeanor.
  • 西上江陵 - Refers to the direction and destination of the journey.
  • 途经江夏 - Indicates that the journey passed through Jiangxia.
  • 韶接信甚薄 - Refers to the subject's cold reception of the other party.
  • 引信入宴 - Refers to the subject inviting the other party to a banquet.
  • 坐信别榻 - Indicates that the other party was seated on a separate couch.
  • 稍不堪 - Indicates that the other party became increasingly uncomfortable.
  • 径上韶床 - Refers to the other party climbing onto the subject's bed.
  • 直视韶面 - Indicates that the other party looked directly at the subject's face.
  • 宾客满座 - Indicates that there were many guests present.
  • 甚惭耻 - Refers to the subject feeling very ashamed.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 弥子瑕 - A historical figure, a favored official of the state of Wei.
  • 卫国 - Refers to the state of Wei, one of the major states during the Warring States period.
  • 灵公 - Refers to Duke Ling of Wei, the ruler of the state of Wei.
  • 王韶 - A historical figure, a high-ranking official during the Southern Dynasties period.
  • 郢州 - Refers to Jingzhou, a region in ancient China.
  • 庾开府 - Likely refers to a high-ranking official or nobleman, possibly from the Southern Dynasties period.

Proper Nouns

  • 弥子瑕 - A historical figure, a favored official of the state of Wei.
  • 卫国 - Refers to the state of Wei, one of the major states during the Warring States period.
  • 灵公 - Refers to Duke Ling of Wei, the ruler of the state of Wei.
  • 王韶 - A historical figure, a high-ranking official during the Southern Dynasties period.
  • 郢州 - Refers to Jingzhou, a region in ancient China.
  • 庾开府 - Likely refers to a high-ranking official or nobleman, possibly from the Southern Dynasties period.

[1477]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 色慕兵子 - 色慕 means to admire or be attracted to someone, so this phrase indicates that the market boy admires or is attracted to '兵子.'
  • 无地与狎 - 无地 means 'no place' or 'nowhere,' and 狎 means to be intimate or familiar. Together, it suggests that the market boy has no opportunity to be intimate with '兵子.'
  • 出入门者，必籍记之，甚严 - 出入门者 refers to people entering or leaving, 籍记之 means to record them, and 甚严 means 'very strict.' This phrase describes the strict record-keeping at the granary.
  • 代未到者名 - 代未到者名 means to take the name of someone who hasn't arrived, suggesting that the market boy impersonates someone else.
  • 玩月 - 玩月 means to enjoy the moon, indicating that the beautiful person is admiring the moon.
  • 调之 - 调之 means to flirt or tease someone.
  • 大怒 - 大怒 means to become very angry.
  • 语斗不已 - 语斗 means to argue or quarrel, and 不已 means 'continuously.' This phrase indicates that the argument between the market boy and the beautiful person continues.
  • 殴美者死 - 殴 means to beat, 美者 means 'the beautiful person,' and 死 means 'to die.' This phrase describes the market boy beating the beautiful person to death.
  • 弃尸井中 - 弃尸 means to throw the body away, and 井中 means 'into the well.' This phrase describes the market boy throwing the body into the well.
  • 君为我至，义不可忘 - 君 is a respectful term for 'you,' 为我至 means 'come for me,' and 义不可忘 means 'the sense of duty cannot be forgotten.' This phrase indicates that '兵子' is asking the market boy to come and help him because of their sense of duty.
  • 我当代君死 - 我当代君死 means 'I will die in your place.' This phrase indicates that '兵子' is willing to take the blame and die for the market boy.
  • 君可应我名出矣 - 君可应我名出矣 means 'You can respond to my name and come out.' This phrase suggests that '兵子' is asking the market boy to come out by responding to his name.
  • 囹圄中愿相顾也 - 囹圄 means 'prison,' and 愿相顾也 means 'I hope you will look after me.' This phrase indicates that '兵子' hopes the market boy will take care of him in prison.
  • 渠虽负义，非我初心 - 渠 means 'he,' 虽负义 means 'although he has betrayed me,' and 非我初心 means 'it is not my original intention.' This phrase indicates that '兵子' is saying that although the market boy has betrayed him, it is not what he originally intended.
  • 我终不令渠死我独生耳 - 我终不令渠死我独生耳 means 'I will not let him die while I live alone.' This phrase indicates that '兵子' is determined to die with the market boy.
  • 触木死尸傍 - 触木 means 'to hit a tree' or 'to strike a tree,' and 死尸傍 means 'beside the dead body.' This phrase describes '兵子' striking a tree and dying beside the market boy's body.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 兵子 - A name or nickname, possibly a soldier or someone with a military background.
  • 百夫长 - A military rank, equivalent to a centurion in the Roman army.
  • 通州仓 - A granary in Tongzhou, likely a significant storage facility for grain.
  • 《耳谈》 - A historical or literary text that records this story. *Er Tan* is a collection of anecdotes and stories.

Proper Nouns

  • 兵子 - A name or nickname, possibly a soldier or someone with a military background.
  • 百夫长 - A military rank, equivalent to a centurion in the Roman army.
  • 通州仓 - A granary in Tongzhou, likely a significant storage facility for grain.
  • 《耳谈》 - A historical or literary text that records this story. *Er Tan* is a collection of anecdotes and stories.

[1478]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 颇有异意 - Literally 'different intention,' here it likely means 'resentment' or 'anger.'
  • 朝中暮三时食 - Refers to the daily meals of morning, noon, and evening.
  • 伴我宿 - To keep me company during the night.
  • 除服作祭 - To remove mourning attire and perform a sacrifice.
  • 食尽 - When the food is finished.
  • 号泣 - To weep loudly.
  • 儿还 - Refers to the return of a child or someone resembling a child.
  • 断绝 - To cease or break off.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 台书佐 - A position in the imperial bureaucracy, indicating the social status of the characters.
  • 令史 - A lower-ranking official, often involved in administrative duties.
  • 嘉兴 - A city in Zhejiang, known for its historical significance and cultural heritage.
  • 棺殡 - The traditional Chinese practice of burying the dead, involving the use of a coffin.
  • 除服作祭 - A ritual involving the removal of mourning attire and the performance of a sacrifice, often part of the mourning process.
  • 食尽 - The completion of a meal, often used in classical texts to indicate the end of an event.
  • 号泣 - A strong emotional response, often associated with grief or sorrow.
  • 儿还 - A term that can refer to the return of a child or someone resembling a child, often used in contexts of supernatural appearances.
  • 断绝 - To cease or break off, often used to describe the end of a relationship or interaction.

Proper Nouns

  • 任怀仁 - The name of the protagonist.
  • 台书佐 - The position of the protagonist.
  • 令史 - The position of Wang Zu.
  • 王祖 - The name of the antagonist.
  • 嘉兴 - A city in Zhejiang.
  • 徐祚家 - The family of Xu Zuo.
  • 除服作祭 - A ritual involving the removal of mourning attire and the performance of a sacrifice.
  • 食尽 - The completion of a meal.
  • 号泣 - Weeping loudly.
  • 儿还 - The return of a child or someone resembling a child.
  • 断绝 - To cease or break off.

[1479]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 身及父母兄弟皆故倡也 - Ruan Yan and his parents, siblings were all former entertainers. '故' refers to former or past, and '倡' means entertainers or performers.
  • 坐法腐刑 - Ruan Yan was subjected to castration as punishment for breaking the law. '坐法' means being punished by law, and '腐刑' refers to castration.
  • 给事狗监中 - Ruan Yan was assigned to work in the Dog Supervisor's office. '给事' means to serve or work, and '狗监' is the office of the Dog Supervisor, a low-ranking official responsible for dogs in the imperial household.
  • 善歌，为新变声 - Ruan Yan was skilled in singing and composed new styles of music. '善' means skilled or proficient, '歌' means singing, and '新变声' refers to new and varied musical styles.
  • 是时方兴天地诸祠 - At that time, various temples dedicated to heaven and earth were being built. '是时' means at that time, '方兴' means just beginning, and '天地诸祠' refers to temples dedicated to heaven and earth.
  • 令司马相如等作诗颂 - Emperor Wu ordered Sima Xiangru and others to compose poems and odes. '令' means to order, '司马相如' is Sima Xiangru, a famous poet, and '诗颂' means poems and odes.
  • 延年辄承意弦歌 - Ruan Yan would follow the emperor's intentions and play music. '辄' means always or immediately, '承意' means to follow the intention, and '弦歌' means to play music.
  • 所造诗为之声曲 - The poems he composed were set to music. '所造' means composed, '为之' means for it, and '声曲' means musical tunes.
  • 而女弟李夫人得幸 - His sister, Lady Li, became favored by the emperor. '女弟' means sister, '李夫人' is Lady Li, and '得幸' means to become favored.
  • 产昌邑王 - She gave birth to Prince Changyi. '产' means to give birth, and '昌邑王' is Prince Changyi, who later became Emperor Zhao.
  • 延年由是贵 - Ruan Yan therefore gained favor and status. '由是' means therefore, and '贵' means to gain favor and status.
  • 为协律都尉 - He was appointed as the Inspector of Music. '为' means to be, '协律' means to harmonize music, and '都尉' is a military rank, but in this context, it refers to a high-ranking official.
  • 佩二千石印绶 - He wore the seals and ribbons of a high-ranking official. '佩' means to wear, '二千石' refers to a high-ranking official, and '印绶' means seals and ribbons.
  • 而与上卧起 - He lived closely with the emperor. '而' means and, '与' means with, '上' means the emperor, and '卧起' means to live closely.
  • 其爱幸将韩嫣 - He was favored by the emperor along with Han Yan. '其' means his, '爱幸' means to be favored, and '将' means along with, '韩嫣' is Han Yan, a favorite of the emperor.
  • 久之，延年弟季与中人乱 - After a long time, Ruan Yan's younger brother, Ji, had an affair with a eunuch. '久之' means after a long time, '延年弟季' means Ruan Yan's younger brother Ji, and '与中人乱' means to have an affair with a eunuch.
  • 及李夫人卒后，其爱驰 - After Lady Li died, his favor declined. '及' means after, '李夫人卒后' means after Lady Li's death, '其爱驰' means his favor declined.
  • 上遂诛延年兄弟宗族 - The emperor then executed Ruan Yan and his family. '上' means the emperor, '遂' means then, '诛' means to execute, and '延年兄弟宗族' means Ruan Yan and his family.
  • 是后宠臣，大底外戚之家也 - After this, the emperor's favorites were mostly from the imperial family. '是后' means after this, '宠臣' means favorites, '大底' means mostly, and '外戚之家' means the imperial family.
  • 卫青、霍去病皆爱幸，然亦以功能自进 - Wei Qing and Huo Qubing were also favored, but they rose to power through their abilities. '卫青' and '霍去病' are Wei Qing and Huo Qubing, famous generals, '皆爱幸' means they were all favored, '然' means but, '亦以功能自进' means they rose to power through their abilities.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 李延年 - Ruan Yan, a musician and poet during the Han Dynasty.
  • 中山人 - A person from Zhongshan, a region in present-day Hebei Province.
  • 倡 - Entertainers or performers, often of low social status.
  • 腐刑 - Castration, a severe punishment in ancient China.
  • 狗监 - The office of the Dog Supervisor, a low-ranking official responsible for dogs in the imperial household.
  • 司马相如 - Sima Xiangru, a famous poet and statesman during the Han Dynasty.
  • 天地诸祠 - Temples dedicated to heaven and earth, reflecting the importance of these deities in ancient Chinese religion.
  • 诗颂 - Poems and odes, often used in religious and ceremonial contexts.
  • 承意弦歌 - Following the emperor's intentions and playing music, indicating the close relationship between the musician and the emperor.
  • 声曲 - Musical tunes, often set to poetry.
  • 李夫人 - Lady Li, a favorite of Emperor Wu and the mother of Prince Changyi.
  • 昌邑王 - Prince Changyi, who later became Emperor Zhao.
  • 协律都尉 - The Inspector of Music, a high-ranking official responsible for music in the imperial court.
  • 二千石 - A high-ranking official, indicating a significant position in the government.
  • 印绶 - Seals and ribbons, symbols of official rank and authority.
  • 韩嫣 - Han Yan, a favorite of Emperor Wu.
  • 中人 - Eunuchs, who often held influential positions in the imperial court.
  • 外戚 - Members of the emperor's family, particularly those related by marriage.
  • 卫青 - Wei Qing, a famous general during the Han Dynasty.
  • 霍去病 - Huo Qubing, another famous general during the Han Dynasty.

[1480]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 一雌复一雄，双飞入紫宫 - A metaphor for the relationship between Fu Jian and the princess, where '一雌复一雄' means 'one female and one male,' and '双飞入紫宫' means 'both flew into the Purple Palace' (a metaphor for the imperial palace).
  • 皇太后有令 - An imperial edict issued by the empress dowager, indicating a formal command.
  • 君臣束手早迭皇帝 - A phrase meaning 'ruler and ministers surrender and quickly take the emperor,' indicating a call to action.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 慕容冲 - Murong Chong, a prince of the Later Yan dynasty, who played a significant role in the fall of the Former Qin.
  • 秦主苻坚 - Fu Jian, the emperor of the Former Qin, known for his ambitious conquests.
  • 清河公主 - A title given to Murong Chong's sister, indicating her noble status.
  • 龙阳之姿 - An idiom referring to a man with a beautiful appearance, often used to describe a man who is attractive to women.
  • 长安 - The capital city of the Former Qin, modern-day Xi'an.
  • 王猛 - A prominent advisor to Fu Jian, known for his wisdom and strategic advice.
  • 锦袍 - A brocade robe, a symbol of high status and favor.
  • 皇太后 - The empress dowager, a powerful figure in the court.

Proper Nouns

  • 慕容冲 - Murong Chong, a prince of the Later Yan dynasty.
  • 秦主苻坚 - Fu Jian, the emperor of the Former Qin.
  • 清河公主 - The title of Murong Chong's sister.
  • 王猛 - A prominent advisor to Fu Jian.
  • 锦袍 - A brocade robe.
  • 皇太后 - The empress dowager.

[1481]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 世家子 - Refers to descendants of prominent families, often with a connotation of nobility or high social status.
  • 美如好女 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'beautiful like a good woman,' used to describe physical attractiveness.
  • 弱不胜衣 - Means 'too weak to bear the weight of one's clothes,' indicating frailty or delicacy.
  • 尤善修饰 - Means 'especially skilled in decoration or adornment,' suggesting a focus on appearance and refinement.
  • 经坐处，如荀令之留香也 - A metaphor comparing the scent left behind after sitting, to that of Xun Ling, a famous historical figure known for his fragrance. This implies a lingering, pleasant impression.
  • 出入比目 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'to move about together like the eyes of a fish,' indicating close companionship or inseparability.
  • 连名 - Means 'having the same name' or 'being listed together,' often used in the context of examinations or official records.
  • 欢好无倦 - Means 'to enjoy each other's company without tiring,' indicating a deep and enduring relationship.
  • 犯血症 - Refers to contracting a blood disease, often a euphemism for a serious illness or death.
  • 汤药 - Means 'soup and medicine,' referring to traditional Chinese remedies.
  • 衣不解带 - Means 'to not take off one's clothes,' indicating constant vigilance and care.
  • 疾革 - Means 'the disease has worsened,' indicating a critical condition.
  • 目视千仞不能言 - Means 'looking at Qianren without being able to speak,' indicating a state of despair or inability to communicate.
  • 吾当终身无外交，以此报汝 - Means 'I will remain loyal to you for the rest of my life, as a way to repay you,' indicating a vow of fidelity.
  • 亦效汝死法 - Means 'I will also follow your example in death,' indicating a promise to die in the same manner.
  • 点头含泪而逝 - Means 'nodding with tears and passing away,' indicating a poignant and emotional death.
  • 哀毁过于伉俪 - Means 'grieving more than a married couple,' indicating an extreme level of mourning.
  • 半载 - Means 'half a year,' indicating a short period of time.
  • 叫门声甚急 - Means 'the sound of knocking at the door was very urgent,' indicating a sudden and intense event.
  • 小传 - Means 'short biography,' referring to a brief account of someone's life.
  • 楷书见慰 - Means 'to write in regular script to comfort,' indicating a request for a written message in a formal style.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 张幼文与张千仞 - Refers to two individuals, Zhang Youwen and Zhang Qianren, who are described in the text.
  • 荀令之留香 - Refers to Xun Ling, a historical figure known for his pleasant scent, used here as a metaphor for a lasting impression.
  • 朱生 - Refers to Zhu Sheng, another individual mentioned in the text.
  • 伯起先生 - Refers to the elder brother of Zhang Qianren, known as 'Boqi' or 'Elder Brother Qi.'
  • 八大 - Refers to Zhang Qianren, who is the eighth son in his family, hence his nickname 'Ba Da' (Eight Great).

Proper Nouns

  • 张幼文 - Zhang Youwen, one of the main characters.
  • 张千仞 - Zhang Qianren, one of the main characters.
  • 朱生 - Zhu Sheng, another character mentioned in the text.
  • 伯起先生 - The elder brother of Zhang Qianren, known as 'Boqi' or 'Elder Brother Qi.'
  • 八大 - Zhang Qianren, who is the eighth son in his family, hence his nickname 'Ba Da' (Eight Great).
  • 《金刚经》 - The Diamond Sutra, a significant Buddhist text.

[1482]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 好外 - Likes external affairs or is fond of outside matters.
  • 抚摩周恤，无所不至 - Caress and care for without limit or to the utmost extent.
  • 特煞（矢差）看 - Especially difficult to look at.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 伯起先生 - A proper name, likely referring to a historical or literary figure.
  • 吴语 - Refers to the Wu dialect, a regional dialect spoken in parts of eastern China, particularly in Jiangsu and Zhejiang provinces.
  • 新郎 - Refers to a groom, a term used in traditional Chinese weddings.
  • 家公 - Refers to a father-in-law, a term used in traditional Chinese family relationships.
  • 家婆 - Refers to a mother-in-law, a term used in traditional Chinese family relationships.

Proper Nouns

  • 伯起先生 - A proper name, likely referring to a historical or literary figure.
  • 倪生 - A proper name, referring to the young man named Ni.

[1483]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 遂烝 - Refers to a taboo relationship, specifically with a stepmother or mother-in-law.
  • 如死鸟 - Metaphor for a place far away or abandoned, likely referring to a remote location.
  • 已 - Means 'afterward,' indicating a sequence of events.
  • 遂 - Indicates a sequence of events.
  • 作乱 - Means 'to rebel,' indicating a conspiracy.
  • 献孟于齐 - Means 'offered Meng to Qi,' likely part of a diplomatic exchange.
  • 过宋野 - Means 'passed through the countryside of Song.'
  • 歌之曰 - Means 'they sang, saying,' introducing a folk song or proverb.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 宋朝 - A historical figure, likely a noble or official in the state of Wei during the Spring and Autumn period.
  • 卫灵公 - Duke Ling of Wei, a ruler of the state of Wei during the Spring and Autumn period.
  • 襄夫人宣姜 - The wife of Duke Ling of Wei, also known as Xuan Jiang.
  • 南子 - Another wife of Duke Ling of Wei, known for her influence and controversial reputation.
  • 齐豹 - Likely a high-ranking official or noble who conspired with Song Chao.
  • 北宫喜 - Likely a high-ranking official or noble who conspired with Song Chao.
  • 褚师圃 - Likely a high-ranking official or noble who conspired with Song Chao.
  • 彭水 - A river in ancient China, likely near the state of Wei.
  • 太子蒯瞆 - The crown prince of Wei, likely involved in a political or diplomatic event.
  • 孟 - Likely a person or object being offered to Qi, possibly as part of a diplomatic exchange.
  • 宋野 - The countryside or rural area of Song, likely referring to a region near the state of Wei.

Proper Nouns

  • 宋朝 - The name of the person, Song Chao.
  • 卫灵公 - The title and name of the ruler, Duke Ling of Wei.
  • 襄夫人宣姜 - The title and name of the wife, Xuan Jiang.
  • 南子 - The name of the wife, Nanzi.
  • 齐豹 - The name of the official or noble, Qi Bao.
  • 北宫喜 - The name of the official or noble, Beigong Xi.
  • 褚师圃 - The name of the official or noble, Chushi Pu.
  • 彭水 - The name of the river, Peng River.
  • 太子蒯瞆 - The name of the crown prince, Kui Gui.
  • 孟 - The name of the person or object being offered to Qi, Meng.
  • 宋野 - The name of the rural area, Song's countryside.

[1484]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 越罗衫袂迎春风 - Describes a luxurious robe made of Yue silk, worn in the spring breeze.
  • 玉刻麒麟腰带红 - Describes a finely crafted red belt with a qilin buckle.
  • 楼头曲宴仙人语 - Describes a banquet at the top of a building where the conversation is as ethereal as that of immortals.
  • 帐底吹笙香雾浓 - Describes a scene where the sound of the sheng is accompanied by a thick, fragrant mist.
  • 人间酒暖春茫茫 - Describes a warm spring day where the world is filled with the warmth of wine.
  • 花枝入帘白日长 - Describes a scene where flower branches are seen through the curtains on a long, bright day.
  • 飞窗复道传筹饮 - Describes a scene where drinking cups are passed around through flying windows and elevated walkways.
  • 午夜铜盘腻烛黄 - Describes a scene where a bronze tray with thick, unburned wax is lit by a yellow candle at midnight.
  • 秃衿小袖调鹦鹉 - Describes a scene where a person with a plain collar and short sleeves is training a parrot.
  • 紫绣麻霞蹯哮虎 - Describes a scene where a tiger with a purple embroidered paw is roaring.
  • 折桂销金待晓筵 - Describes a scene where a person is waiting for a dawn banquet while holding a branch of the osmanthus tree.
  • 白鹿青苏半夜煮 - Describes a scene where white deer and herbs are being boiled at midnight.

  • 冀大起第舍，而寿亦对街为宅，殚极土木，互相夸竞 - Describes how He Jin built grand mansions, and He Jin’s brother He Shou also constructed luxurious residences across the street, competing in opulence.
  • 时人谓之木妖 - Metaphorically refers to the excessive construction as a 'wooden monster' or 'wooden demon,' symbolizing the extravagance and potential downfall.
  • 大将军霍光监奴冯子都，有殊色，光爱幸之 - Describes how Feng Zidu, a eunuch of Grand General Huo Guang, was exceptionally beautiful and favored by Huo Guang.

  • 光卒，显寡居，与子都乱 - Describes how after Huo Guang’s death, his wife Huo Xian, who was a widow, had an affair with Feng Zidu.
  • 显广治第室，作乘舆辇，加画绣裀。冯黄金涂韦，絮荐轮 - Describes how Huo Xian decorated her residence lavishly, including embroidered cushions and gold-painted carriages.
  • 侍婢以五彩丝挽显及子都，游戏第中 - Describes how Huo Xian’s maidservants played with her and Feng Zidu using colorful threads in her residence.

  • 戴从之 - '从之' means 'followed him.'
  • 至一处，云是舅居，已具酒饭 - '至一处' means 'arrived at a place,' '云是舅居' means 'said it was the uncle's residence,' and '已具酒饭' means 'already prepared food and drink.'
  • 饭毕，亦有人往来议价 - '饭毕' means 'after the meal,' and '亦有人往来议价' means 'there were also people coming and going to negotiate the price.'
  • 良久，都去，已而寂然 - '良久' means 'for a long time,' '都去' means 'everyone left,' and '已而寂然' means 'and then it became quiet.'
  • 戴入内视之，空屋耳 - '戴入内视之' means 'Wang went inside to look,' and '空屋耳' means 'it was just an empty house.'
  • 急归家，则妇人已尽室行矣 - '急归家' means 'hurried back home,' '则' means 'then,' '妇人已尽室行矣' means 'the woman had already taken everything with her.'
  • 家人云：“舅来言：价已议定，但彼家以非戴原产，必欲娘子自来也。” - '家人云' means 'the family said,' '舅来言' means 'the uncle came to say,' '价已议定' means 'the price has been agreed upon,' '但彼家以非戴原产' means 'but the other party said it was not originally from Wang,' '必欲娘子自来也' means 'they insisted that the wife come herself.'
  • 戴惘然无措，连访数日，不得其踪 - '戴惘然无措' means 'Wang was at a loss,' '连访数日' means 'visited several times,' and '不得其踪' means 'could not find her.'
  • 方悟骗局，叹息弥日 - '方悟骗局' means 'realized it was a scam,' '叹息弥日' means 'sighed all day.'
  • 二事俱贪色之害，并记之 - '二事俱贪色之害' means 'both incidents were due to the harm of greed for beauty,' and '并记之' means 'and recorded them together.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 韩闻之心动，狂不自制 - Han was moved upon hearing it and became so excited that he could not control himself; '心动' (心动) means 'moved emotionally,' and '狂不自制' (狂放不能自制) means 'wild and uncontrollable.'
  • 即欲见之 - Immediately wanted to see her.

  • 韩心益急 - Han became even more anxious.
  • 司谏曩在妾家最善舞，今日能为妾舞一曲，即当出也 - Han, formerly known as the Censor, was best at dancing at my place; if he can dance a piece for me today, I will come out.
  • 韩醉甚，不知所以 - Han was very drunk and didn't know why; '甚' (非常) emphasizes the degree of intoxication.
  • 即索舞衫，涂抹粉墨，踉跄而起，忽跌于地 - He immediately fetched his dance attire, applied makeup, staggered to his feet, and suddenly fell to the ground; '踉跄' (摇摇晃晃) describes unsteady movement.
  • 王亟命索舆，诸娼扶掖而登 - The king quickly ordered a sedan chair to be brought, and the courtesans helped him onto it; '亟' (急忙) means 'hurriedly.'
  • 归船，昏然酣寝 - Returned to the boat and fell into a deep, drunken sleep; '昏然' (昏昏沉沉) describes a state of drowsiness.
  • 五更酒醒，觉衣衫拘绊 - When he woke up at the fifth watch of the night, he felt his clothes were tight; '五更' (fifth watch) is a traditional Chinese time division.
  • 索烛览镜，羞愧无以自容 - Lit a candle to look in the mirror and felt ashamed beyond measure; '无以自容' (无法面对自己) means 'unable to face oneself.'
  • 即解舟还台，不敢复有所问 - Immediately untied the boat and returned to the platform, dared not ask anything further.
  • 此声流播，旋遭弹劾，王迄善罢 - When this news spread, he was soon accused and impeached, and the king eventually let the matter drop; '旋' (很快) means 'soon,' and '迄善罢' (最终善罢甘休) means 'ultimately let the matter drop.'
  • 一个美人计，韩熙载用之，文潞公用之，王鈇复用之，而堕其术中，鲜得脱者 - A beauty trap that Han Xizai, Wen Luxiang, and Wang Fu all fell for, and few escaped its trap; '美人计' (beauty trap) is a common term for a seduction scheme.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 三春桃李本无言 - Literally 'In the three springs, peach and plum trees are naturally silent,' implying that these trees are inherently beautiful without needing to speak. This is a metaphor for natural beauty and inherent worth.
  • 苦被伤残鸟雀喧 - Literally 'Sadly, they are troubled by the noisy birds.' This suggests that the natural beauty of the trees is marred by the disruptive presence of birds.
  • 借问东邻效西子 - Literally 'Asking the east neighbor to emulate the west lady.' This is a metaphorical way of asking someone to imitate a beautiful or virtuous person.
  • 何如郭素学王轩 - Literally 'How about Guo Su learning from Wang Xuan?' This is another metaphorical reference, comparing Guo Su to Wang Xuan, who is implied to be a model of virtue or beauty.
  • 或言王轩乃吴王后身也 - Literally 'Some say that Wang Xuan is the reincarnation of Wu Wang.' This introduces a mythological or legendary element, suggesting that Wang Xuan has a divine or historical significance.
  • 安知刘导又非王轩之后身乎 - Literally 'How can we know that Liu Dao is not the reincarnation of Wang Xuan?' This extends the idea of reincarnation to Liu Dao, further emphasizing the mystical or legendary nature of the characters.
  • 邪鬼假托 - Literally 'Evil spirits pretending.' This suggests that the events or descriptions might be fabricated or influenced by supernatural forces.
  • 未必真也 - Literally 'It is not necessarily true.' This indicates skepticism about the authenticity of the story.
  • 政和改元，七月之望 - Literally 'In the first year of Zhenghe, on the full moon of July.' This provides a specific historical context, indicating the time period when the events took place.
  • 士人杨彦采，陆升之 - Literally 'Scholars Yang Yanci and Lu Shengzhi.' These are proper names of the individuals involved in the story.
  • 载酒出游莲塘 - Literally 'Carrying wine to tour the lotus pond.' This describes the activity of the scholars.
  • 舟回日夕，夜泊横桥下 - Literally 'As the boat returned at dusk, they anchored under the horizontal bridge at night.' This sets the scene for the evening encounter.
  • 月色明霁 - Literally 'The moonlight was bright and clear.' This describes the beautiful night sky.
  • 酒各半酲 - Literally 'Each had half-drunk.' This indicates that the scholars were slightly intoxicated.
  • 闻邻船有琵琶声 - Literally 'They heard the sound of a pipa from a neighboring boat.' This introduces the musical element of the story.
  • 意其歌姬舟也 - Literally 'They thought it was a boat of a singing girl.' This suggests that the scholars assumed the source of the music was a courtesan.
  • 见灯下一姬，自弄弦索 - Literally 'They saw a lady under a lamp, playing her strings herself.' This describes the woman they encountered.
  • 二人径往见之 - Literally 'The two men went straight to see her.' This shows their curiosity and interest.
  • 询其所由 - Literally 'They asked her about her background.' This indicates that the scholars inquired about the woman's story.
  • 答曰：“妾大都乐籍供奉女也。” - Literally 'She replied, 'I am a lady from the entertainment quarters of the capital.' This explains her background.
  • 从人来游江南 - Literally 'I came to tour Jiangnan with my companions.' This provides more context about her journey.
  • 值彼往云间收布 - Literally 'Just as he went to Yunjian to collect cloth.' This explains why she was alone.
  • 妾独处此候之，尚未回也 - Literally 'I am waiting for him here, and he has not returned yet.' This explains her current situation.
  • 二人命取舟中餕馀，肴核 - Literally 'The two men ordered some leftover food from their boat.' This shows their hospitality.
  • 就灯下同酌 - Literally 'They drank together under the lamp.' This describes the intimate setting.
  • 姬举止闲雅，姿色媚丽 - Literally 'The lady's demeanor was leisurely and elegant, her appearance charming and beautiful.' This describes her character and appearance.
  • 二人情动于中，稍挑谑之 - Literally 'The two men were moved by her, and they began to tease her slightly.' This shows their growing interest and playful interaction.
  • 姬亦不以为嫌 - Literally 'She did not find it objectionable.' This indicates her willingness to engage.
  • 求其歌以侑觞 - Literally 'They asked her to sing to accompany their drinking.' This shows their desire for entertainment.
  • 二人强之，乃曰：“近日游访西子陈迹，得古歌数首，敢奉清尘，不讶为荷。” - Literally 'The two men insisted, and she said, 'Recently, I visited the traces of Xi Shi and obtained several ancient songs. I dare to offer them to you, and I hope you will not be surprised.' This explains why she offered to sing.
  • 凡一歌，侑饮一觞 - Literally 'For each song, they drank one cup of wine.' This describes the ritual of their interaction.
  • 歌曰 - Literally 'The song goes:' This introduces the actual lyrics of the song.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 三春桃李 - Refers to the beauty of peach and plum blossoms in spring, symbolizing natural beauty and inherent worth.
  • 西子 - Refers to Xi Shi, one of the Four Great Beauties of ancient China, known for her exceptional beauty.
  • 王轩 - A figure mentioned in the text, possibly a historical or legendary character.
  • 吴王 - Refers to Wu Wang, a historical figure, possibly a king of the Wu state.
  • 刘导 - A figure mentioned in the text, possibly a historical or legendary character.
  • 郭素 - A figure mentioned in the text, possibly a historical or legendary character.
  • 政和改元 - Refers to the first year of the Zhenghe era, a period in Chinese history.
  • 莲塘 - Refers to a lotus pond, a common setting in Chinese literature.
  • 琵琶 - A traditional Chinese musical instrument.
  • 歌姬 - Refers to a courtesan or singing girl.
  • 乐籍 - Refers to the entertainment quarters of the capital, where courtesans and entertainers were based.
  • 江南 - Refers to the southern region of China, known for its natural beauty and cultural significance.
  • 云间 - Refers to a place, possibly a region or city.
  • 西子陈迹 - Refers to the traces or memories of Xi Shi, emphasizing the historical and cultural significance.

[1542]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 風動荷花水殿香 - A poetic phrase describing the fragrance of lotus flowers stirred by the wind in a waterside pavilion.
  • 姑蘇台上宴吳王 - Refers to a feast held on the Terrace of Kuaiji for King Wu of Wu.
  • 西施醉舞娇无力 - Describes Xi Shi dancing in a state of intoxication, her movements graceful yet weak.
  • 笑倚东窗白玉床 - Xi Shi laughs and leans against an eastern window, sitting on a white jade bed.
  • 吳王舊國水烟空 - Refers to the former kingdom of Wu, now desolate and shrouded in mist.
  • 香徑无人兰叶红 - The fragrant path is empty, but the orchid leaves remain red.
  • 春色似怜歌舞地 - The spring scenery seems to sympathize with the place of music and dance.
  • 年年先发馆娃宫 - Every year, the spring arrives first at the Gouwawa Palace.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 姑蘇台 - A famous terrace in ancient China, built by King Wu of Wu, known for its grandeur and beauty.
  • 吳王 - Refers to King Wu of Wu, a historical figure from the Spring and Autumn period.
  • 西施 - A legendary beauty who was given to King Fu Chai of Wu as a gift, often associated with the story of the fall of Wu.
  • 館娃宮 - A palace built by King Fu Chai of Wu for Xi Shi, located on the Tai Lake in Suzhou.
  • 香徑 - A fragrant path, often associated with the beauty and elegance of the past.

Proper Nouns

  • 姑蘇台 - Terrace of Kuaiji.
  • 吳王 - King Wu of Wu.
  • 西施 - Xi Shi.
  • 館娃宮 - Gouwawa Palace.
  • 香徑 - Fragrant Path.

[1543]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 無風自偃 - Idiomatic expression suggesting something happens spontaneously or naturally, without external force.
  • 西子裙裾拂過來 - Poetic metaphor where Xi Shi's skirt is used to symbolize the movement of the wind or a gentle breeze.
  • 半夜娃宮作戰場 - Metaphorical expression, where the palace is transformed into a battlefield, likely to convey a sense of chaos or destruction.
  • 血腥猶雜宴時香 - Vivid and ironic description, contrasting the horror of battle with the remnants of a past feast.
  • 西施不及燒殘燭 - Metaphorical expression, where the burning candle symbolizes the fading of time or memory.
  • 夜夜蘇台空月明 - Poetic description, using the moonlight to evoke a sense of solitude or emptiness.
  • 舊苑荒台楊柳新 - Metaphorical expression, where the new willows contrast with the ruined structures, symbolizing the passage of time.
  • 菱歌清唱不勝春 - Poetic comparison, where the singing is compared to the beauty of spring.
  • 只今惟有西江月 - Metaphorical expression, where the moon symbolizes the only thing left unchanged over time.
  • 家國興亡來有以 - Philosophical statement, suggesting that the fate of a nation is often unpredictable or beyond human control.
  • 吳人何苦怨西施 - Rhetorical question, challenging the common belief that Xi Shi was responsible for the downfall of the Wu state.
  • 西施若解亡吳國 - Conditional statement, questioning Xi Shi's role in the state's downfall.
  • 越國亡來又是誰 - Rhetorical question, pointing out that the blame for the fall of Yue should not be placed solely on Xi Shi.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 館娃宮 - The Palace of Guanwa, located in Suzhou, was the palace of King Fuchai of Wu during the Spring and Autumn period. It is a significant historical site associated with the story of Xi Shi.
  • 蘇台 - The Terrace of Suzhou, another historical site in Suzhou, often mentioned in literature as a symbol of beauty and elegance.
  • 西施 - A famous beauty from the state of Yue, who was given to King Fuchai of Wu as a concubine. She is often blamed for the downfall of the Wu state, though this is a controversial view.
  • 吳國 - The state of Wu, one of the major states during the Spring and Autumn period, which was eventually conquered by the state of Yue.
  • 越國 - The state of Yue, which conquered Wu and became a powerful state in its own right.
  • 西江月 - The moon over the West River, often used in poetry to evoke a sense of timelessness or melancholy.

Proper Nouns

  • 館娃宮 - The Palace of Guanwa.
  • 蘇台 - The Terrace of Suzhou.
  • 西施 - Xi Shi, the famous beauty.
  • 吳國 - The state of Wu.
  • 越國 - The state of Yue.
  • 西江月 - The moon over the West River.

[1544]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 家是红萝亭上仙 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'the home is like a fairy on the red pavilion,' likely referring to a beautiful or ethereal place.
  • 谪来尘世已多年 - Literary phrase meaning 'exiled to the mortal world for many years,' suggesting a long period of living among humans.
  • 君心既逐东流水 - Poetic expression meaning 'your heart has followed the east-flowing water,' symbolizing the passing of time or the impermanence of emotions.
  • 却把无缘当有缘 - Philosophical statement meaning 'yet treating what is unconnected as connected,' suggesting a misunderstanding or misinterpretation of relationships.
  • 掀篷揽衣跃入水中 - Vivid description of a person quickly removing their clothing and jumping into the water, possibly indicating a sudden or desperate action.
  • 汗背而觉，一梦境也 - Phrase meaning 'sweating on the back and realizing it was a dream,' indicating the realization that the experience was not real.
  • 寻升之共话 - Phrase meaning 'seeking to talk with him,' suggesting a desire for conversation or understanding.
  • 醉眠脚后，不能寝也 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'sleeping drunk at the foot of the bed, unable to sleep,' indicating a state of restlessness or discomfort.
  • 翌日，事传吴下 - Phrase meaning 'the next day, the matter spread to Wu,' indicating the rapid dissemination of news.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 红萝亭 - Poetic reference to a beautiful or ethereal place, possibly a pavilion or garden.
  • 尘世 - Term meaning 'mortal world,' often used in contrast to a spiritual or heavenly realm.
  • 东流水 - Metaphor for the passage of time or the impermanence of life, often used in Chinese poetry.
  • 无缘 - Term meaning 'no connection' or 'no relationship,' often used in Buddhist or Taoist contexts.
  • 吴下 - Term referring to the region of Wu, a historical area in southeastern China.
  • 昭君 - Famous historical figure, Wang Zhaojun, who was sent to marry a foreign ruler to maintain peace.
  • 牛僧孺 - Historical figure, Niu Sengru, a prominent scholar and politician during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 真元中 - Period during the Tang Dynasty, specifically the reign of Emperor Xianzong.
  • 进士 - Term referring to a successful candidate in the imperial examination, a key part of the Chinese civil service system.
  • 宛叶间 - Region in Henan Province, historically known for its cultural and political significance.
  • 汉文帝 - Historical figure, Emperor Wen of Han, known for his benevolent rule and reforms.
  • 薄太后 - Historical figure, Empress Dowager Bo, the mother of Emperor Wen of Han.

Proper Nouns

  • 昭君 - Wang Zhaojun, a famous historical figure.
  • 牛僧孺 - Niu Sengru, a historical figure.
  • 汉文帝 - Emperor Wen of Han, a historical figure.
  • 薄太后 - Empress Dowager Bo, a historical figure.

[1545]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 此是庙，郎不当来，何辱至此 - Polite refusal meaning 'This is a temple, you should not be here, why do you disgrace yourself by coming here?'
  • 臣家宛下，将归失道，恐死豺虎，敢乞托命 - Humble plea for protection meaning 'My home is in the south, I am lost on the way back, afraid of being killed by tigers, I dare to ask for your protection.'
  • 妾故汉室老母，君唐朝名士，不相君臣 - Refusal to treat each other as superior and subordinate meaning 'I am an old mother of the Han dynasty, you are a famous scholar of the Tang dynasty, we are not in a master-servant relationship.'
  • 行役无苦乎 - Polite inquiry about one's well-being meaning 'Are you not tired from your journey?'
  • 屈两娘子出见秀才 - Command to bring out the two ladies to meet the scholar meaning 'Bring out the two ladies to meet the scholar.'
  • 迎杨家潘家来 - Command to bring the Yang and Pan families meaning 'Bring the Yang and Pan families.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 庙 - Refers to a temple, a place of worship.
  • 汉室老母 - Refers to an old woman from the Han dynasty, indicating her age and status.
  • 唐朝名士 - Refers to a famous scholar from the Tang dynasty, indicating his status and era.
  • 高祖戚夫人 - Refers to Lady Qi, a concubine of Emperor Gaozu of Han, known for her beauty and tragic fate.
  • 元帝王嫱 - Refers to Wang Zhaojun, a famous beauty who was sent to the Xiongnu as a peace offering, known for her loyalty and sacrifice.
  • 杨家潘家 - Refers to the Yang and Pan families, likely prominent families of the time.

Proper Nouns

  • 庙 - Temple.
  • 汉室老母 - Old mother of the Han dynasty.
  • 唐朝名士 - Famous scholar of the Tang dynasty.
  • 高祖戚夫人 - Lady Qi, concubine of Emperor Gaozu of Han.
  • 元帝王嫱 - Wang Zhaojun, a famous beauty sent to the Xiongnu.
  • 杨家潘家 - Yang and Pan families.

[1546]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 久之 - A temporal phrase indicating a passage of time.
  • 空中見五色云下 - A vivid description of a colorful cloud descending from the sky, possibly with a supernatural connotation.
  • 聞笑語聲寖近 - Indicates that the sound of laughter and conversation gradually approaches, suggesting an auditory experience.
  • 太后曰 - A formal way of introducing the speech of the empress dowager.
  • 楊潘至矣 - A statement indicating that Yang Pan has arrived, with '至矣' emphasizing the arrival.
  • 忽車音馬跡相雜 - Describes the sudden presence of the sounds of carriages and hoofprints, indicating movement.
  • 羅绮焕耀 - Describes the bright and dazzling appearance of silk robes, likely worn by the figures arriving.
  • 旁視不給 - Suggests that the observer is overwhelmed by the sight, unable to fully take it in.
  • 有二女子從雲中下 - Indicates that two women descend from the cloud, adding to the supernatural element.
  • 余起立於側 - Describes the narrator standing up beside the others, showing respect or anticipation.
  • 見前一人纖腰修眸，容甚麗 - A detailed description of a person with a slender waist, long eyes, and beautiful appearance.
  • 衣黄衣，冠玉冠 - Describes the attire of the person, with a yellow robe and a jade crown, suggesting a high status.
  • 年三十許 - Indicates that the person is around thirty years old.
  • 此是唐朝太真妃子 - Identifies the person as Yang Guifei, a famous concubine of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 余即伏謁，拜如臣禮 - Describes the narrator bowing deeply, as if to a subject, showing respect.
  • 豈不虛乎 - A rhetorical question suggesting that the gesture is unnecessary or meaningless.
  • 不敢受，卻答拜 - Describes the narrator refusing the bow and instead returning the gesture.
  • 更一人，厚肌敏視，小質潔白，齒極卑，被寬博衣 - A detailed description of another person with a plump body, sharp eyes, small stature, white complexion, and low teeth, wearing a loose robe.
  • 齊潘淑妃 - Identifies the person as a noblewoman from the Qi state, known for her beauty and status.
  • 既而太后命進饌 - Indicates that the empress dowager orders food to be served.
  • 少時，饌至 - Describes the food arriving shortly after.
  • 芳潔萬端 - Describes the food as fragrant and pure in countless ways.
  • 皆不得名字 - Suggests that the food items are unnamed or unknown.
  • 但欲充腹，不能足食 - Indicates that the food is sufficient to satisfy hunger but not in abundance.
  • 已更具酒 - Describes the preparation of wine.
  • 其器用盡如王者 - Indicates that the utensils and vessels are of royal quality.
  • 何久不來相看 - A question asking why the person has not visited for a long time.
  • 謹容對曰 - Describes a formal and respectful response.
  • 三郎數幸華清宮，扈從不得至 - Explains that the person has been accompanying the emperor to the Huaqing Palace and thus has not been able to visit.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 太后 - Refers to the empress dowager, a high-ranking figure in the imperial court.

  • 唐朝太真妃子 - Refers to Yang Guifei, a famous concubine of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, known for her beauty and influence.
  • 齊潘淑妃 - Refers to a noblewoman from the Qi state, known for her beauty and status.
  • 華清宮 - Refers to the Huaqing Palace, a famous imperial retreat during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 三郎 - Likely refers to Emperor Xuanzong of Tang, who was also known as the 'Third Son' (三郎).

Proper Nouns

  • 齊潘淑妃 - A noblewoman from the Qi state, known for her beauty and status.
  • 華清宮 - The Huaqing Palace, a famous imperial retreat during the Tang Dynasty.

[1547]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 子亦不来，何也？ - Direct question asking why someone is not coming, with '子' referring to a person.
  • 匿笑不禁 - Describes a person who cannot stop laughing, indicating a hidden or suppressed amusement.
  • 懊恼东昏侯疏狂 - '懊恼' means 'disappointed' or 'regretful,' '东昏侯' refers to a historical figure, and '疏狂' describes a carefree and unrestrained lifestyle.
  • 不得时谒耳 - '不得时谒' means 'unable to visit at the right time,' and '耳' is a particle indicating a statement or explanation.
  • 今天子为谁？ - A question asking who the current emperor is.
  • 先帝长子 - Refers to the eldest son of the previous emperor.
  • 沈婆儿作天子也，大奇！ - '沈婆儿' is likely a nickname or title, and '大奇' means 'very strange' or 'very unusual.'
  • 何如主？ - A question asking about the quality of the ruler.
  • 民间传圣武 - '民间' means 'among the people,' '传' means 'to say' or 'to report,' and '圣武' refers to the emperor's martial prowess.
  • 首肯三四 - '首肯' means 'nodding in agreement,' and '三四' indicates several times.
  • 盍各赋诗言志 - '盍' is a particle suggesting an invitation, '各' means 'each,' '赋诗' means 'to compose poetry,' and '言志' means 'to express one's aspirations.'
  • 逡巡，诗成 - '逡巡' means 'in a short while' or 'after a moment,' and '诗成' means 'the poems are completed.'
  • 薄后诗曰 - '薄后' likely refers to a historical figure, and '诗曰' means 'the poem says.'
  • 王嫱诗曰 - '王嫱' refers to Wang Qiang, a historical figure, and '诗曰' means 'the poem says.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 太后 - Refers to the empress dowager, a high-ranking female figure in the imperial court.
  • 潘妃 - Likely refers to a historical or fictional female figure associated with the empress dowager.
  • 太真 - A title or nickname, likely referring to a person of high status.
  • 东昏侯 - Refers to Emperor Houzhu of Southern Qi, known for his extravagant and reckless behavior.
  • 先帝 - Refers to the previous emperor.
  • 沈婆儿 - Likely a nickname or title for the current emperor, possibly Emperor Wen of Sui.
  • 汉家旧是笙歌处 - Refers to the historical significance of the Han dynasty, known for its cultural and musical achievements.
  • 烟草几经秋复春 - A metaphorical expression indicating the passage of time and the cyclical nature of seasons.

Proper Nouns

  • 先帝 - The previous emperor (likely referring to Emperor Wu of Liang)
  • 沈婆儿 - A nickname or title for the current emperor (likely referring to Emperor Wen of Sui)

[1548]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 汉衣虽旧泪痕新 - Poignant expression indicating that although the Han clothing is old, the tears are fresh, symbolizing enduring sorrow.

  • 金钗堕地别君王 - Metaphorical expression describing the falling of a golden hairpin, symbolizing separation from the king.
  • 红泪流珠满御床 - Metaphorical expression describing the shedding of red tears, symbolizing intense sorrow.
  • 云雨马嵬分散后 - Metaphorical expression describing the separation after the events at Ma嵬 (a place associated with a historical tragedy).
  • 骊宫不复舞霓裳 - Metaphorical expression describing the inability to dance the *Ni Chang* (a famous dance) in the palace.
  • 秋月春风几度归 - Metaphorical expression describing the passage of time through the cycles of autumn and spring.
  • 江山犹是汉宫非 - Metaphorical expression indicating that the landscape remains the same, but the palace is no longer the Han palace.
  • 东昏旧作莲花地 - Metaphorical expression referring to the past when the east was a place of lotus flowers.
  • 空想会披金缕衣 - Metaphorical expression describing the vain hope of wearing golden-threaded clothes.
  • 香风引到大罗天 - Metaphorical expression describing the fragrant wind leading to the highest heaven.
  • 月地云阶拜洞仙 - Metaphorical expression describing the act of worshiping a cave immortal on the moonlit earth and cloud-studded steps.
  • 尽道人间惆怅事 - Metaphorical expression indicating that everyone speaks of the sorrows of life.
  • 不知今夕是何年 - Metaphorical expression expressing the confusion of time, wondering what year it is.
  • 石家绿珠也 - Direct statement identifying the person as Lu Zhu, a famous beauty from the Stone Family.
  • 潘妃养作妹 - Direct statement indicating that the person was raised as a sister by Pan Fei.
  • 红残翠碎花楼下 - Metaphorical expression describing the decay of the red and green colors under the flower pavilion.

  • This sentence uses the metaphor of mandarin ducks to express the rarity of separation in a loving relationship, where '鸳鸯匹对' (a pair of mandarin ducks) and '未闻经月离也' (unheard of for a month) are key elements.

  • 后五日，会之七夕 - Meet on the seventh day of the seventh month, referring to the Qixi Festival.
  • 设珍果，焚香相待 - Prepare precious fruits and burn incense to wait, describing a ritualistic or ceremonial preparation.
  • 仍屏左右 - Keep the attendants away, indicating a request for privacy.
  • 复闻荷叶声，乃不见 - Again hearing the sound of lotus leaves, then she disappeared, describing a mysterious and sudden disappearance.
  • 容服益华美于前 - Her appearance and attire were even more splendid than before, indicating a significant improvement in her appearance.
  • 何不精若此 - Why not be as meticulous as this? Expressing surprise and criticism.
  • 大人性严 - My master is strict, indicating a high standard of behavior.
  • 极力止此耳 - Did my best to prepare this, indicating limited resources or effort.
  • 女令青衣取于其家 - The girl ordered the maid to fetch from her home, indicating a request for additional items.
  • 若只此池畔取之 - As if it was just fetched from this pond, indicating the ease of the task.
  • 所赍果实 - The fruits she brought, referring to the items she carried.
  • 市廛中物 - Things from the market, indicating common or everyday items.
  • 每食留其核，置砚匣内 - Every time she ate, she kept the seeds and placed them in the inkstone box, indicating a ritual or habit.
  • 夜分同寝 - Slept together at night, indicating a close relationship.
  • 率如常人 - Behaved like an ordinary person, indicating normal behavior.
  • 切勿轻泄，使我受祸 - Do not reveal this lightly, it will bring me trouble, indicating a warning.
  • 家人讶韩病瘁 - The family was surprised by Han's illness and exhaustion, indicating a change in his condition.
  • 庄敏公移官陕右 - Zhuang Min Gong was transferred to the western region of Shaanxi, indicating a significant event.
  • 我所不能以逐君去者 - The reason I cannot follow you away is, indicating a limitation.
  • 道途修阻，弱质弗堪 - The road is long and difficult, and my weak body cannot endure it, indicating physical limitations.
  • 相别之后，幸无念我，且得罪 - After we part, please do not miss me, and do not blame me, indicating a farewell and a request for understanding.
  • 岂能无念哉 - How can I not miss you? Indicating a strong emotional response.
  • 遂别 - And they parted, indicating the end of their interaction.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 七夕 - The Qixi Festival, a traditional Chinese festival celebrating the meeting of the Cowherd and Weaver Girl, symbolizing love and fidelity.
  • 焚香 - Burning incense, a common ritual in Chinese culture for purification and communication with the spiritual world.
  • 青衣 - A maid or servant, often depicted in traditional Chinese literature and art.
  • 市廛 - A market or marketplace, indicating a place where goods are bought and sold.
  • 砚匣 - An inkstone box, a traditional writing implement used for grinding ink.
  • 庄敏公 - A title given to a high-ranking official, indicating a person of significant status.
  • 陕右 - The western region of Shaanxi, a historical and geographical reference.

Proper Nouns

  • 韩 - A person's name, indicating a specific individual.
  • 庄敏公 - A title given to a high-ranking official, indicating a person of significant status.
  • 七夕 - The Qixi Festival, a specific cultural event.
  • 陕右 - The western region of Shaanxi, a specific geographical location.

  • 若有所感 - Means 'as if feeling something,' suggesting an emotional or spiritual experience.

  • 共白父母庄敏公 - Means 'together report to the parents, the honorable and quick-witted official.'
  • 杖之 - Means 'to hit with a stick,' often used metaphorically to mean scolding or disciplining.

[1684]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 筑庵山中 - Literally 'build a hermitage in the mountains,' referring to the act of establishing a retreat in a mountainous area.
  • 径入卧室 - Literally 'enter the bedroom directly,' indicating a bold or unexpected entry.
  • 自解下体 - Literally 'undress her lower body,' suggesting an intimate or provocative action.
  • 各榻而寝 - Literally 'sleep on separate beds,' indicating a physical separation despite being in the same room.
  • 轻如一叶 - Literally 'light as a leaf,' a metaphor for something extremely light or insubstantial.
  • 经从佛出 - Literally 'the scripture comes from the Buddha,' emphasizing the divine origin of the scripture.
  • 佛岂在经耶 - Literally 'is the Buddha in the scripture?' questioning the idea that the Buddha can be confined to written text.
  • 打得人心碎 - Literally 'strike one's heart to pieces,' a metaphor for causing emotional pain.
  • 髻上梳掠鬓而去 - Literally 'comb her hair and leave,' indicating a graceful departure.
  • 妾住小水边 - Literally 'I live by the small water,' referring to the speaker's residence near a body of water.
  • 君住青山下 - Literally 'you live under the green mountain,' referring to the addressee's residence in a mountainous area.
  • 青山不可再 - Literally 'the green mountain cannot be seen again,' suggesting a sense of loss or absence.
  • 日月坐成夜 - Literally 'the sun and moon turn into night,' a metaphor for the passage of time or the transition from day to night.
  • 源谷里 - Literally 'in the source of the valley,' referring to a place in a valley.
  • 风雨误芳年 - Literally 'wind and rain miss the fragrant year,' suggesting a missed opportunity or a delay in a beautiful time.
  • 薄情君抛弃 - Literally 'you, the unfeeling one, abandoned me,' expressing a sense of betrayal or abandonment.
  • 咫尺万里远 - Literally 'a distance of ten thousand miles in a footstep,' a metaphor for a great distance despite physical closeness.
  • 月空明 - Literally 'the moon is empty and bright,' suggesting a clear and unobstructed moon.
  • 芭蕉心不展 - Literally 'the banana heart does not unfold,' a metaphor for a closed or unexpressed emotion.

[1685]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 解下绿罗裙，无情对有情 - Poetic expression where removing a green silk skirt symbolizes a departure from emotional attachment, contrasting '无情' (unfeeling) with '有情' (feeling).
  • 那知妾意重，只道妾身轻 - Idiomatic expression indicating the speaker's feelings are deep and significant, but others perceive her as being of little value.
  • 经从佛口出，佛不在经里 - Paradoxical statement suggesting that while teachings come from the Buddha, the essence of the Buddha is not confined to the scriptures.
  • 郎在妾心头，郎身隔万里 - Metaphorical expression indicating that although the beloved is physically far away, they remain deeply in the heart of the speaker.
  • 月色照罗衣，永夜不得寐 - Poetic description of the moonlight illuminating the speaker's attire, leading to insomnia.
  • 莫打五更钟，打得人心碎 - Plea to avoid the sound of the dawn bell, as it would cause emotional distress.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 绿罗裙 - A green silk skirt, often associated with elegance and femininity in classical Chinese literature.
  • 佛口出，佛不在经里 - References to Buddhist teachings and the idea that true wisdom transcends written texts.
  • 五更钟 - The dawn bell, traditionally rung at the fifth watch of the night, signaling the start of a new day.

Proper Nouns

  • 无 - No proper nouns in this passage.
